re_line: Kim commissions Choe Hyon destroyer, orders nuclear navy · DPRK plenum vows exponential arsenal · SK Hynix Nasdaq debut · ROK PM hearing · Korean War anniversary
morning_memo: Kim Jong Un commissioned the Choe Hyon, North Korea's first 5,000-ton destroyer, and ordered construction of two warships per year for five years as part of a declared nuclear navy buildup.
SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via a Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10, in what would be the largest foreign company debut on US markets in years.

Prime Minister nominee Han Seong-sook faced a two-day parliamentary confirmation hearing where she described North Korea as 'both a threat and a compatriot,' drawing opposition fire over her eligibility amid controversies.

on_this_day.event: North Korean forces crossed the 38th parallel, launching the Korean War — a conflict that killed an estimated 36,000 Americans, over 130,000 South Korean troops, and millions of civilians before the armistice in July 1953.
on_this_day.relevance: President Lee Jae-myung marked the 76th anniversary today at a ceremony in Gyeonggi Province, pledging to build a peaceful Korean Peninsula free from fear of war — framed against the backdrop of Kim Jong Un's simultaneous declaration of a nuclear navy expansion.

key_stat.label: SK Hynix planned Nasdaq fundraise (target: Jul 10)
key_stat.context: SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via a Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10, which would represent one of the largest foreign company IPOs in US market history and reflects surging global demand for HBM memory chips.
key_stat.source: CNBC — South Korea's biggest chipmaker SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10

imagery_report.label: Korean Peninsula Update — AEI
imagery_report.headline: AEI releases Korean Peninsula Update for June 23, 2026
imagery_report.body: AEI published its latest Korean Peninsula Update on June 23, 2026, covering recent activity at monitored DPRK facilities. The update coincides with Kim Jong Un's commissioning of the Choe Hyon destroyer and Workers' Party plenum declarations on nuclear expansion. Specific facility findings from the AEI report are not fully detailed in today's feed summaries, but the update was published the day before the destroyer commissioning ceremony.

bp_locations.name: Yongbyon Nuclear Complex
bp_locations.note: Apr 2026: Enrichment building expansion complete, new centrifuge hall operational. 5 MWe reactor and IRT-2000 both running. Thermal signatures consistent with increased plutonium/HEU production. AEI's June 2026 special report on DPRK constitutional amendments notes the nuclear program's posture is now formally entrenched in state law.

bp_locations.name: Sinpo South Shipyard
bp_locations.note: Feb 2025: Hero Kim Kun Ok SLBM submarine exited drydock after 2+ years of fitting-out but still not operational as of May 2025. New submarine construction begun in separate bay.

bp_locations.name: THAAD Site — Seongju County
bp_locations.note: Jun 21, 2026: All six THAAD launchers returned to the permanent Seongju site from Osan Air Base, restoring the battery's full deployment footprint. Battery operational status remains subject to ongoing assessment after the partial Middle East redeployment.

bp_locations.name: Sohae Satellite Launch Station
bp_locations.note: May 2026: Rocket engine test conducted last week, possibly for military reconnaissance satellite program revival. New viewing area building nearing completion. Previous reports showed 2,500 kN engine test stand firing and facility expansion.

bp_locations.name: Punggye-ri Nuclear Test Site
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: ROK DIA assessed Tunnel No. 3 is test-ready and a nuclear test can be conducted at any time upon political decision. Site maintained at high readiness since mid-2024.

bp_locations.name: Tumangang–Khasan (NK-Russia border)
bp_locations.note: Jun 19, 2026: Satellite imagery shows new Tumen River vehicle bridge nearing completion with finishing touches underway but customs infrastructure not yet complete on either side; scheduled opening June 19, 2026. The Economist (Jun 21) reports the bridge is nearly complete. Previous reporting: new road bridge construction accelerated, completion target moved to Summer 2026. Passenger rail service resumed mid-2025. Ore and tank car traffic at highest levels since project began.

bp_locations.name: Sinuiju–Dandong (NK-China border)
bp_locations.note: Mar 12, 2026: Cross-border passenger train service resumed between Sinuiju and Dandong after COVID-era suspension. New Yalu River Bridge may open in 2026.

bp_locations.name: Rason SEZ
bp_locations.note: Nov 2025: Chinese businesspeople re-entry to Rason SEZ expanded. Coal exports through Rason at highest levels in years throughout 2025. Arms export berth also active.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea NLL
bp_locations.note: Sep 26, 2025: 140m DPRK merchant vessel Toksong crossed NLL near Baengnyeong Island, advanced 5km south. ROK Navy fired ~60 warning shots; vessel retreated after 1 hour. Ship switched AIS nationality to China and flew Chinese flag. First NLL intrusion in three years.

bp_locations.name: Yellow Sea PMZ
bp_locations.note: Atlantic Amsterdam (converted oil rig, Chinese aquaculture platform) relocated out of PMZ Jan 27-28, 2026 to Weihai shipyard following Lee Jae-myung Beijing state visit. Two giant aquaculture cages (Shen Lan 1, Shen Lan 2) and 13 buoys remain in the PMZ.

bp_locations.name: Vostochny/Dunai (Russian Far East)
bp_locations.note: Jan 2026: Arms shipments slowed due to ice — one Russian vessel (likely Angara or Lady R) visited Rason, docked Jan 14 at arms export berth. Cargo routed to Vostochny. Since Sep 2023: 64 voyages by 4 vessels delivered ~15,800 containers from Rajin to Vostochny/Dunai.

rok_government.action: Lee delivers Korean War anniversary address vowing peaceful peninsula
rok_government.detail: President Lee Jae-myung spoke at a ceremony in Gyeonggi Province marking the 76th anniversary of the Korean War's outbreak, pledging to 'safeguard the people and the territory through strong defense capabilities.' He said a peaceful Korean Peninsula free from fear of war would be 'the surest reward' for veterans who sacrificed their lives.

rok_government.action: Vice defense minister lobbies US Congress on nuclear-powered subs and OPCON transfer
rok_government.detail: Vice Defense Minister Lee Doo-hee separately met with Representatives Ryan Zinke (R-MT) and Pat Harrigan (R-NC) in Washington on Wednesday, requesting congressional support for Korea's nuclear-powered submarine program and the transfer of wartime operational control (OPCON) to Seoul before Lee Jae-myung's term ends in 2030. Seoul is pursuing low-enriched uranium fuel sourcing under a summit agreement.

rok_government.action: MND announces tech-intensive NCO track as part of conscription reform
rok_government.detail: South Korea plans a new noncommissioned officer track specializing in manned-unmanned systems, cyber operations, and artificial intelligence, with four-to-five year service terms, as part of the Lee administration's push for a 'selective voluntary enlistment system.' The initiative aims to augment mandatory service with professionally trained tech specialists.

rok_government.action: PM announces government conclusion to abolish prosecution's investigative rights entirely
rok_government.detail: Prime Minister Kim Min-seok stated Thursday that the government has reached a final conclusion that the prosecution should not retain supplementary investigative rights, aligning with the ruling Democratic Party's push to abolish prosecutorial investigative power entirely. The announcement slightly diverged from President Lee Jae-myung's earlier cautious stance on negative effects of full abolition.

top_stories.headline: Kim Jong Un commissions Choe Hyon destroyer, orders 10-warship nuclear navy buildup over five years
top_stories.body: Kim Jong Un commissioned the Choe Hyon, a 5,000-ton destroyer described as North Korea's largest-ever warship, at a ceremony in the West Sea, and directed that two warships be built annually for the next five years — targeting a fleet that would include 10,000-ton missile cruisers. At a concurrent Workers' Party Central Committee second plenary meeting, Kim declared North Korea will 'exercise its position as a nuclear state' and ordered 'exponential' expansion of the nuclear arsenal. Kim also reaffirmed 'full support and solidarity' for Russian forces in Ukraine.
top_stories.so_what: The commissioning converts the plenum's doctrinal declarations into a concrete force-structure directive; watch for KCNA publication of the full plenum resolution and any announcement of 10,000-ton cruiser construction timelines.
top_stories.pattern_note: The CSIS provocation database records a MIRV capability test on Jun 26, 2024 — the day after this anniversary — suggesting the Korean War date carries deliberate escalatory timing for Pyongyang.
top_stories.src_line: WaPo · Reuters · AP · NYT · NK News · CNN

top_stories.headline: SK Hynix targets $29 billion Nasdaq listing as soon as July 10
top_stories.body: SK Hynix plans to raise $29 billion via a Nasdaq debut targeting as early as July 10, according to CNBC, in what would rank among the largest foreign company listings in US market history. The WSJ confirmed the planned Nasdaq debut. The listing reflects surging demand for SK Hynix's high-bandwidth memory chips used in AI applications, though analysts note the weak Korean won clouds the near-term earnings outlook.
top_stories.so_what: A successful $29B listing would significantly expand SK Hynix's US investor base and dollar-denominated capital access ahead of potential new US semiconductor investment commitments.

top_stories.headline: PM nominee Han Seong-sook begins two-day confirmation hearing; calls North Korea 'threat and compatriot'
top_stories.body: Prime Minister nominee Han Seong-sook appeared before the National Assembly for her two-day confirmation hearing, telling opposition lawmakers who asked about South Korea's main enemy that North Korea is 'both a threat and a compatriot' — a framing that drew scrutiny from conservative lawmakers. Han, currently minister for small and medium-sized enterprises and startups, also faces controversy over a personal data leak in a government startup program and ownership of multiple properties. She described the next few years as a critical period for South Korea's AI competitiveness.
top_stories.so_what: Han's confirmation vote will determine whether President Lee Jae-myung secures a cooperative prime minister to advance the prosecution reform and defense modernization agendas simultaneously.

overnight_items.headline: Irkutsk business delegation arrives in Pyongyang pitching fuel, fertilizers, and construction
overnight_items.body_text: A large Russian business delegation from the Irkutsk region arrived in North Korea this week, with representatives of five companies offering fuel, fertilizers, agricultural products, and construction services to the DPRK market, according to the East Siberian Chamber of Commerce. The visit expands the civilian commercial dimension of the Russia-DPRK relationship alongside the military cooperation axis, occurring in the same week as Kim Jong Un pledged full support for Russian forces in Ukraine at the Workers' Party plenum.

overnight_items.headline: Nordwind Airlines trains Air Koryo staff on ticketing systems inside DPRK, heralding expanded Russia flights
overnight_items.body_text: Russia's Nordwind Airlines partnered with a Moscow-based IT service to train North Korean Air Koryo staff on ticketing systems inside the DPRK, according to a Nordwind press release. Nordwind is the first company in decades to operate direct flights between Russia and North Korea; the training arrangement is likely a precursor to increased North Korean arrivals in Russia, potentially including labor workers whose travel would require expanded ticketing capacity.

overnight_items.headline: UN Command: North Korean DMZ construction is civil, not an armistice violation
overnight_items.body_text: The UN Command issued a fact sheet Tuesday stating that recent North Korean construction inside the Demilitarized Zone does not violate the 1953 Korean War Armistice Agreement, specifying the projects remain on the northern side of the Military Demarcation Line and have not altered the military balance in the buffer zone. The UNC clarification comes as South Korea marks the 76th anniversary of the Korean War's outbreak, with President Lee Jae-myung simultaneously visiting a shelled island and calling for deterrence.

overnight_items.headline: Korea postpones multipurpose satellite Arirang 6 launch to 2027 after European partner setback
overnight_items.body_text: South Korea has postponed the launch of its multipurpose satellite Arirang 6 until 2027 following a setback by its European development partner. The delay affects Seoul's civilian Earth-observation capability and comes as the DPRK accelerates its own military reconnaissance satellite program, with Sohae showing engine test activity as recently as May 2026.

overnight_items.headline: South Korea misses MSCI developed market watchlist again on market access concerns
overnight_items.body_text: South Korea has again failed to gain inclusion on the MSCI developed market watchlist, with MSCI citing unresolved market access concerns. The repeated exclusion — driven by offshore foreign exchange trading restrictions and corporate governance issues — constrains the pool of passive index-tracking capital available to Korean equities, a structural headwind that policymakers have sought to address through the 'Korea Discount' reform agenda.

overnight_items.headline: Ex-minister says Japan and South Korea 'only have each other' as US reliability wavers
overnight_items.body_text: A former minister quoted in the South China Morning Post argued that Japan and South Korea must deepen their bilateral relationship because US reliability is no longer assured, stating the two countries 'only have each other.' The framing reflects anxiety in both capitals about the durability of Trump administration alliance commitments, adding diplomatic pressure to an already fraught Japan-ROK relationship navigating history disputes and security cooperation imperatives.

also_today.headline: US Ambassador to UN calls DPRK abductions 'state-sponsored kidnapping,' urges resolution
also_today.body_text: US Ambassador to the UN Mike Waltz called North Korea's past abductions of Japanese citizens 'state-sponsored kidnapping' and urged Pyongyang to immediately resolve the decades-old cases, stating the US stands with Japan, South Korea, and all nations whose citizens were 'stolen by the DPRK.'

also_today.headline: Satellite imagery detects new activity at DPRK military parade training base
also_today.body_text: NK Pro analysis of Planet Labs satellite imagery shows North Korea's main military parade training base has become active again, suggesting Pyongyang may hold another parade in 2026 — following large-scale parades in October 2025 and February 2026, each requiring months of formation practice for over 10,000 troops. Potential timing would coincide with DPRK founding day (Sep 9) or Workers' Party founding day (Oct 10).

also_today.headline: Russia supplies DPRK with Ebola test kits at Pyongyang's request as health cooperation expands
also_today.body_text: Russia delivered Bundibugyo variant Ebola test kits to North Korea at Pyongyang's request, according to a June 19 press statement from a Moscow-based government agency, expanding bilateral health cooperation alongside military and economic ties. The transfer occurred as a new Ebola outbreak emerged in Africa.

also_today.headline: Kia union seeks veto power over corporate decisions as Korea's new labor law fallout deepens
also_today.body_text: The Kia Motors union is seeking veto power over key corporate decisions following the passage of Korea's new labor law, deepening the governance tension between management and labor at major chaebols. The fallout from the new labor legislation is testing boardroom authority across Korea's industrial conglomerates.

also_today.headline: Foreign tourists to South Korea hit record 10 million in first half, card spending up 47%
also_today.body_text: Inbound foreign tourists to South Korea reached a record 10 million in the first half of 2026, with card spending by visitors up 47% year-on-year, according to data reported by Korea Economic Daily. The figures reflect continued post-pandemic recovery in Korean tourism and favorable won depreciation effects for inbound visitors.

business_economy.headline: Samsung and SK Hynix to announce massive facility investments as Korea maps second chip cluster
business_economy.body_text: Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix are expected to announce major facility investments as South Korea plans a second semiconductor cluster, building on the existing Yongin-area hub. The announcements would form part of Seoul's broader industrial policy to anchor advanced chipmaking capacity domestically amid US-China technology competition.

business_economy.headline: GS Group in talks with South Korean government to build two 1.2 GW data centers
business_economy.body_text: GS Group is in discussions with the South Korean government to build two data centers each with 1.2 gigawatts of capacity, reflecting the accelerating AI infrastructure build-out in Korea. The scale of the proposed facilities underscores the power grid demands now shaping Korea's industrial and energy policy.

business_economy.headline: South Korea's AI chip boom spills into property market
business_economy.body_text: Demand for data center and semiconductor fab real estate is reshaping South Korea's commercial property market, driven by AI chip investment from Samsung, SK Hynix, and their supply chain partners, according to Nikkei Asia. Land prices and industrial leasing costs near Yongin and other cluster sites have surged as domestic and foreign investors compete for infrastructure-ready plots.

business_economy.headline: Korea's SK Hynix to make Nasdaq debut
business_economy.body_text: The Wall Street Journal reported SK Hynix's planned Nasdaq listing, confirming CNBC reporting on the July 10 target date. The listing would give the world's second-largest memory chipmaker direct access to US equity capital markets.

business_economy.headline: Ministry of Land approves Korean Air-Asiana merger; integrated airline to launch December 17
business_economy.body_text: South Korea's Ministry of Land, Infrastructure and Transport approved the Korean Air-Asiana Airlines merger with conditions, setting December 17 as the launch date for the integrated carrier. The merger, years in the making, consolidates Korea's two full-service airlines into a single flag carrier with a substantially expanded international route network.

northeast_asia.headline: Sejong Institute: Significance and Tasks of the Korea-China Summit Talks
northeast_asia.body_text: South Korea's Sejong Institute published analysis on the significance and forward tasks of the recent Korea-China summit, examining how Seoul can leverage the diplomatic opening with Beijing while managing THAAD-related economic sensitivities. The analysis frames the summit as a structural reset opportunity but warns of persistent friction over the US-ROK alliance.

northeast_asia.headline: Ukraine and Japan discuss risks of Russia-DPRK cooperation and energy support
northeast_asia.body_text: Japanese and Ukrainian officials held discussions on the risks posed by Russian-DPRK military cooperation, alongside energy support issues, according to Ukrainian National News Agency (UNN). The bilateral consultation reflects Japan's growing concern about North Korean munitions flowing to Russian forces and the implications for the broader rules-based order.

northeast_asia.headline: South Korea warns Canada could favor NATO member in submarine bid
northeast_asia.body_text: South Korean officials have warned that Canada may favor a NATO-member competitor in its submarine procurement competition, undercutting Seoul's bid despite offering competitive technology. The concern adds diplomatic pressure to Korea's export-driven defense industry push at a moment when the DPRK's new destroyer underscores domestic naval ambitions.

gallup_spotlight.finding: 67% of respondents assessed election management as inadequate, and 25% said the ballot shortage constituted evidence of electoral fraud, per Gallup Korea's second-week June survey.

rok_personnel.name: Han Seong-sook
rok_personnel.detail: Han Seong-sook, currently serving as minister for small and medium-sized enterprises and startups, underwent the first day of a two-day parliamentary confirmation hearing on Thursday. She faces controversies over a personal data leak in a government startup program and ownership of multiple properties.

rok_assembly.action: Two-day confirmation hearing opens for PM nominee Han Seong-sook
rok_assembly.detail: The main opposition People Power Party questioned Han's eligibility on grounds including a personal information leak and multiple property ownership. Han described AI competitiveness as a defining national challenge and said North Korea is 'both a threat and a compatriot.'

rok_assembly.action: Investigators question election officials over June 3 ballot shortage
rok_assembly.detail: A joint police and prosecution team summoned two Songpa District Election Commission officials to question them about internal instructions during the ballot shortage on election day, probing whether the National Election Commission hastily reduced printed ballot numbers. Nine local government officials who distributed ballots at shortage-hit Seoul polling stations were also scheduled for questioning.

opeds_today.headline: Anthropic's Export Control Crackdown Leaves South Korea Caught in Washington's AI Crossfire
opeds_today.central_argument: US export controls on advanced AI models, shaped by Silicon Valley lobbying, are imposing collateral costs on allied economies like South Korea that lack leverage in Washington's AI policy process.
opeds_today.summary: The piece examines how Anthropic's suspension of its most advanced AI model under US export control rules has affected South Korean access to frontier AI technology. It argues that US export policy is being driven by competitive corporate interests rather than allied coordination. Seoul's dependence on US AI platforms creates a structural vulnerability in Korea's AI competitiveness.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Relevant to the ROK government's AI competitiveness agenda and ongoing US-Korea technology cooperation discussions, including any expansion of the Washington Declaration technology annex.

opeds_today.headline: How Far Can South Korea's Export Rally Go?
opeds_today.central_argument: South Korea's export recovery is real but faces structural headwinds from won weakness, global demand deceleration, and US tariff risk that could truncate the rally before it translates into sustained GDP gains.
opeds_today.summary: KEIA's analysis examines the drivers and limits of South Korea's recent export surge, assessing semiconductor, auto, and petrochemical export performance. The piece maps the sustainability question against US tariff risk — particularly the Section 122 surcharge expiry on July 24 and the ongoing Section 301 investigation. It identifies the weak Korean won as a double-edged factor: boosting export competitiveness while increasing dollar-denominated input costs.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Informs US-Korea trade negotiation posture ahead of the July 24 Section 122 surcharge expiry and any Congressional debate over extending the 150-day authority.

opeds_today.headline: Northeast Asia's New Strategic Equation: US Policy Toward China, North Korea, and the US-ROK Alliance
opeds_today.central_argument: The simultaneous pressure from China's Indo-Pacific posture and North Korea's accelerating nuclear and naval buildup requires the US to articulate a coherent Northeast Asia policy that reinforces rather than trades off the US-ROK alliance against broader China strategy.
opeds_today.summary: Hudson Institute's analysis frames the current strategic environment as a new equation in which DPRK nuclear advancement and Chinese regional assertiveness interact rather than operate in isolation. The piece argues US policy must treat the ROK alliance as a load-bearing pillar of broader China deterrence rather than a cost center. It assesses the implications of Kim Jong Un's nuclear navy declarations for extended deterrence commitments.
opeds_today.policy_so_what: Directly relevant to the NSC's integrated China-DPRK policy review and any forthcoming US-ROK Extended Deterrence Strategy and Consultation Group (EDSCG) meeting agenda.

kcna_delta.kim_activity: Kim Jong Un commissioned the Choe Hyon destroyer — North Korea's largest-ever warship at 5,000 tons — at a West Sea ceremony, and chaired the Workers' Party Central Committee second plenary meeting where he declared DPRK would exercise its position as a nuclear state, ordered construction of two warships annually for five years, and pledged full support for Russian forces in Ukraine.

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea will exercise its position as a nuclear state.
key_quotes.source_article: KCNA via Reuters, AP, WaPo, NK News — Workers' Party Central Committee second plenary meeting, Jun 24, 2026

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea should build two warships a year in next five years.
key_quotes.source_article: KCNA via Reuters — Choe Hyon commissioning ceremony, Jun 24, 2026

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: North Korea will offer full support and solidarity to the Russian army and people in carrying out the special military operation in Ukraine.
key_quotes.source_article: KCNA via AFP/France 24, Workers' Party plenum — Jun 24, 2026

key_quotes.speaker: Kim Jong Un
key_quotes.quote: Expanding nuclear arsenal to 'overtake the world.'
key_quotes.source_article: 조선일보 citing KCNA — Workers' Party Central Committee second plenary, Jun 24, 2026

kcna_delta.bottom_line: Kim Jong Un's commissioning of the Choe Hyon destroyer combined with the Workers' Party plenum directive to build ten warships over five years and 'exercise its position as a nuclear state' converts doctrinal rhetoric into a formal force-structure plan; watch for KCNA publication of the full plenum resolution and any timeline announcement for 10,000-ton missile cruiser construction.

calendar_watch.headline: BOK Monetary Policy Board meeting — rate decision
calendar_watch.detail: Bank of Korea's next scheduled rate decision. Current rate stands at 2.50% following the June 2026 adjustment. Markets will be watching for any further easing signals given won weakness and global demand uncertainty.

calendar_watch.headline: Section 122 surcharge expiry — 150-day statutory limit
calendar_watch.detail: The 10% global Section 122 tariff surcharge (effective Feb 24, 2026) expires on July 24 unless Congress extends authority. If no extension, USTR's Section 301 investigations (launched Mar 11, 2026 against South Korea and 15 other trading partners) would become the primary legal basis for new tariff action.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean War Armistice anniversary — 73rd
calendar_watch.detail: The 73rd anniversary of the July 27, 1953 armistice that ended active fighting in the Korean War. Pyongyang typically uses the date — celebrated in North Korea as 'Victory in the Fatherland Liberation War Day' — for military displays or political statements.

calendar_watch.headline: Korean Liberation Day (광복절) — politically significant presidential address
calendar_watch.detail: South Korea's national Liberation Day typically features a major policy address by the president. President Lee Jae-myung's address will be watched for North Korea engagement signals and any update to the inter-Korean policy framework, particularly given Kim Jong Un's nuclear navy declarations this week.

calendar_watch.headline: Ulchi Freedom Shield (UFS) — annual US-ROK combined military exercise (est. mid-August)
calendar_watch.detail: The annual US-ROK combined exercise, typically held for approximately 11 days in mid-August, is expected to proceed. DPRK has historically responded to UFS with heightened rhetoric or provocations; Kim Jong Un's current naval expansion directives add context to this year's exercise environment.

investment_package.latest_update: No new WH-verified deal announced today; National Assembly passed the Special Investment Act (226-8-8) on March 12, 2026, creating the Korea-US Strategic Investment Corporation to implement the $350B pledge.

known_deals.sector: Manufacturing — Georgia EV, Louisiana steel, robotics hub
Engines and maintenance for Boeing fleet
Shipbuilding — Philly Shipyard expansion, 10x capacity increase
Power grid infrastructure — undersea cables, power equipment (incl. LS Cable $681M VA facility)
Critical Minerals — Tennessee smelter, 13 minerals incl zinc/copper/rare earths
Defense — 4 AEW&C aircraft for ROK Air Force
Food and Beverage — Burleson TX plant
Critical Minerals — US rare earth separation/refining

known_deals.company: Samsung Heavy Industries / Vigor Marine Group

trade_policy.detail: Launched Mar 11, 2026 against 16 trading partners including South Korea; could provide new legal authority for tariffs after Section 122 expires Jul 24, 2026.

trade_policy.item: BIS Semiconductor Export Controls — Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs
trade_policy.detail: Samsung and SK Hynix China fabs operating under annual BIS export licenses renewed Dec 30, 2025 for 2026; maintenance and incremental upgrades only, no expansion. Shifted from indefinite VEU waivers to restrictive annual licenses Jan 2026.

trade_policy.item: IRA Battery Sourcing Rules (FEOC) — Korean EV battery makers
trade_policy.detail: Foreign Entity of Concern rules affect LG Energy Solution, SK On, Samsung SDI sourcing from China; battery components phase-in Dec 2024, critical minerals Dec 2025. ACTIVE.

trade_policy.item: CHIPS Act Guardrails — Samsung Taylor TX
trade_policy.detail: Samsung received preliminary $6.4B CHIPS award (Apr 2024) for Taylor TX fabs; 10-year restriction on materially expanding semiconductor manufacturing in countries of concern. Samsung Xi'an NAND fab limited to legacy production.

tariff_tracker.headline_note: 10% Section 122 surcharge (effective Feb 24, 2026) applies to all Korean goods; expires Jul 24, 2026 unless Congress extends; the US-Korea Strategic Trade and Investment Deal's 15% IEEPA reciprocal rate was struck down by the Supreme Court (Feb 20, 2026, 6-3).

sector_rates.note: All country exemptions/TRQs eliminated Mar 2025; 50% applies universally to Korean steel and aluminum with no quota arrangement.
Reduced from 25% under the US-Korea Strategic Trade and Investment Deal; retroactive to Nov 1, 2025 per USTR Dec 3, 2025 implementation notice.
Effective Jan 15, 2026; broader semiconductor tariffs pending Commerce/USTR report; deal promises Korea terms no less favorable than comparable trading partners.
Rate cap agreed in US-Korea deal but specific Section 232 action not yet issued.
Tariffs removed per USTR Dec 3, 2025 notice.

sector_rates.sector: Generic Pharma / Precursors / Rare Natural Resources

tariff_tracker.section_122_surcharge: 10% global surcharge (Section 122, effective Feb 24, 2026, expires Jul 24, 2026)
tariff_tracker.last_change: Feb 24, 2026 — White House imposed 10% Section 122 surcharge on all countries after Supreme Court struck down IEEPA tariffs (Feb 20, 2026, 6-3)
tariff_tracker.next_trigger: Jul 24, 2026 — Section 122 surcharge expiry (150-day statutory limit); Section 301 investigations ongoing (launched Mar 11, 2026)
